transcript.clean_text: if you thought after two hellish days in the mountains were in for an easier time of things on the welter then think again the organizers posting a spectacular stage 191 kilometers to montford lamos and climbing straight out the back door then it was a rolling plateau with plenty of peaks one of which included the intermediate sprint after which it was downhill run to the line 18 went for the break including many of the pre-stage favorites it was high quality to say the least and drew response from the peloton bike exchange did a lot of early chasing taken over by dsm but they weren't working together bike exchange of course hoping to tear up michael matthews and dainese was the choice of dsm even though they had nico dents in the break that break started to disintegrate it wasn't to the liking the work rate anyway of rui oliveira he pushed on a sprinter hoping to shake out some of those he felt were passengers well it turned out to be a seven man breakaway then and as dsm and bike exchange started to work together there was a compression within the pelleted and unfortunately a touch of wheels which took out louis mankeys he'd been bidding for a top 10 place in this welter and would have had designs on it with the penultimate stage out of the race he went well our breakaway started to reconfigure out front and quinn simmons knew he'd have to go long if he was going to have a chance against the sprinters the american doing a great job kron was also there likewise credit and magnus court nielsen rui oliveira anthony wu of francis de gea and badgioli from de kerny quickstep whose man of course in the green jersey fabio jacobson was cruising at the back 16.9 kilometers to go and the gap was down to around about half a minute finally bike exchange and team dsm decided to work together but they were getting too close to home and in so doing it meant that that three kilometer rule was going to draw out the gc teams who were riding for safety with just over eight kilometers to go the margin was still holding and we're building in a little bit of cat and mouse time for the break when would they believe well not until the very last handful of kilometers so they said afterwards so who would win two very very testy right-hand corners in the last 800 meters with the last challenge to the break because the peliton now knew the fate was sealed so who would it be well quintiman said he had to go very very early he pushed on oliveira probably on paper the fastest sprinter here even in the company of someone like magnus court nielsen magnus called of course had lawson credit to lead out meaning that his timing was perfect he delivered beautifully on the line indeed won by half a bike length magnus caught with his third victory of this welter absolutely magnificent well for a team hobbled of course very early on having lost three members including of course hugh carthy who was their gc contender and picked up sprint wins with magnus court coming home behind dynasty crossed the line in front of trenton and krieger but the story had already been told three stage wins on this welter for magnus court nielsen magnus the magnificent joy unbridled for his team and no surprise confirmation of the result what a way to win it 18 seconds was the gap back to the chasing peloton in the end a phenomenal day then high powered full of drama and it'll be the same tomorrow you can be sure primus roglic looking ever more secure in the red jersey especially with the time trial still to come the margin he holds with two stages remaining two minutes and thirty over enric mass that top ten now not to be troubled sadly by louis menkes who's off to hospital so stage 20 castro air v is indeed our ultimate destination and this time the climbs will come towards the end of the day an uphill test indeed on a category two it'll be gnarly you can be sure very little flat to contend with and five climbs that will be categorized for that king of the mountains competition will it be magnificent of course it will be it's love welter for goodness sake
